display.purpose: Dashboard titles and important modal titles only.

headline.purpose: Panel headings, report groups, and case titles.

title.purpose: Rows, alert summaries, and conversation subjects.

body.purpose: Messages, reasoning, summaries, and draft responses.

label.purpose: Navigation, metadata, chips, and compact controls.

shadows.name: crisp-rest
shadows.purpose: Slight separation from the dashboard wash background.

shadows.name: popover-lift
shadows.purpose: Menus, command palette, date pickers, and tooltips.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Buttons, row hovers, tabs, and compact feedback.

motion.name: panel-standard
motion.purpose: Sheets, dropdowns, and expandable evidence panels.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: One primary action per local workflow region.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 36px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; background: #0f766e; color: #ffffff; font: 600 12px/1.2 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0b5f59; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f766e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Default action button for secondary workflow controls.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 36px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #d9dee2; border-radius: 8px; background: #ffffff; color: #182026; font: 600 12px/1.2 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #0f766e; background: #eef8f6; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f766e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Compact semantic label for status, severity, and agent state.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 26px; padding: 5px 9px; border: 1px solid #d9dee2; border-radius: 999px; background: #ffffff; color: #69747c; font: 600 12px/1.2 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-chip-live { border-color: rgba(15, 118, 110, 0.3); background: rgba(15, 118, 110, 0.07); color: #0f766e; }

components.name: Dashboard Card
components.description: Flat manager-facing panel for summaries, reports, and grouped controls.
components.html:
Live agent work

12 conversations active, 3 waiting for manager review.
components.css: .ds-card { width: 280px; padding: 16px; border: 1px solid #d9dee2; border-radius: 12px; background: #ffffff; color: #182026; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(24, 32, 38, 0.08); font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0; color: #182026; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.35; font-weight: 650; } .ds-card p { margin: 8px 0 0; color: #69747c; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.55; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Compact quick link for the manager dashboard sidebar.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; height: 38px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 8px; color: #69747c; text-decoration: none; font: 600 13px/1 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #eef2f4; color: #182026; } .ds-nav-item-active { background: rgba(15, 118, 110, 0.08); color: #0f766e; } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0f766e; outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview: Concierge Court should feel like a dense operations board for hotel leaders: clean, fast, legible, and always oriented around what needs attention. The redesigned dashboard uses a Linear-like product rhythm with a tight sidebar, crisp surfaces, quiet typography, and status-forward rows. It is not a marketing page and not a technical agent console; the interface exists to help managers understand what the AI agents are doing and where humans should step in.
keyCharacteristics: Dense manager-first information layout with compact navigation and fast route switching.
Restrained neutral palette with teal used sparingly for primary actions, current location, and live agent activity.
Status semantics that combine color, label, icon, and shape so severity stays readable for everyone.
shadcn components as the baseline, restyled to fit Concierge Court rather than left at defaults.
Human interception controls are visible, direct, and phrased in hotel operations language.

rules.name: The Ten Percent Accent Rule
rules.body: Teal, violet, coral, and amber are operational signals, not decoration. If a screen feels colorful before the data is read, the color use is too loud.

rules.name: The Status Redundancy Rule
rules.body: Color never carries status alone. Every verdict, severity, confidence, and escalation state must include text and, when useful, an icon.

rules.name: The UI Scale Rule
rules.body: Do not use landing-page type scales in the dashboard. Product headings are fixed rem sizes, not fluid hero typography.

rules.name: The No Jargon Label Rule
rules.body: Labels must describe user-facing work: "Review draft", "Take over chat", "Escalate case", "View evidence". Avoid internal agent-stage names unless the user is inspecting a trace.

rules.name: The Flat Operations Rule
rules.body: Tables, inbox rows, trace items, and dashboard panels stay flat at rest. Depth is used for hierarchy and interaction, not visual softness.

dos: Do use shadcn primitives as the implementation baseline, then tune tokens, density, and state vocabulary for Concierge Court.
Do make manager overview visible before detail: workload, live agents, risky chats, pending approvals, and operations patterns.
Do keep AI actions auditable with clear traces, citations, drafts, tickets, and intervention controls.
Do use the current restraint of Paper, Dashboard Wash, Command Ink, Rule Line, and Operational Teal as the base palette.
Do include keyboard states, skeleton loading, empty states, reduced-motion support, and color-blind-safe status patterns.
donts: Don't make the dashboard feel overly technical, developer-first, or like an AI lab console.
Don't use generic SaaS marketing polish, luxury-hotel beige, decorative AI gradients, glassy effects, or empty dashboard ornament.
Don't hide human intervention. "Take over chat", "Review draft", "Rerun investigation", and "Escalate case" should be visible where the decision is made.
Don't use color alone for severity, verdict, confidence, or escalation state.
Don't create identical card grids with icon, heading, and paragraph repeated across the dashboard.
Don't pair 1px borders with large soft shadows on every surface. Pick crisp border-first layering for normal dashboard UI.
